evals.prompt: I keep having a problem where you modify a function in utils.py and then main.py breaks because you forgot to update the call site. Can you set up something that catches this automatically in my Python project?
evals.expected_output: The skill installs the Ruff + Pyright PostToolUse hook: creates .venv, installs ruff and pyright, copies the bundled scripts to scripts/, configures .claude/settings.json, and verifies with a smoke test.
expectations: A .venv directory is created with ruff and pyright installed
scripts/python_check_hook.py is created in the project
scripts/run_python_hook.sh is created and made executable
.claude/settings.json contains a PostToolUse hook entry with matcher Write|Edit|MultiEdit
A smoke test is performed to verify the hook fires correctly
requirements-dev.txt is created listing ruff and pyright

evals.prompt: Add type checking automation to this project. I want pyright to run after every file edit and tell you if you've broken anything.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets up the full hook pipeline: venv with ruff+pyright, hook scripts, settings.json configuration. Pyright runs project-wide after each edit to catch cross-file type errors.
expectations: A .venv directory is created with ruff and pyright installed
scripts/python_check_hook.py is created in the project
.claude/settings.json contains a PostToolUse hook with command 'bash scripts/run_python_hook.sh'
The hook is verified to catch type errors (smoke test with a deliberate error)

evals.prompt: Set up ruff and pyright hooks for my Python project so code quality is checked automatically when you edit files.
evals.expected_output: Full hook installation: .venv, ruff + pyright installed, bundled scripts copied to scripts/, .claude/settings.json configured with PostToolUse hook, smoke test completed.
expectations: ruff is installed and accessible via .venv
pyright is installed and accessible via .venv
